reasoning: After extensive search of news between May 2 and June 5, 2025, I found: (1) Suarez actively discussed a gubernatorial run through May 7, confirming he's still interested (search_0_summary, search_1_summary). (2) However, he acknowledged Trump's endorsement of Byron Donalds makes it 'hard' and mentioned seeking 'other ways to serve' (search_1_summary). (3) Critically, there is NO news coverage from May 8 through June 5 - four weeks of complete silence on this topic. (4) The election is in 2026, giving him no urgency to announce by July 1, 2025. (5) Historically, he dropped out of the 2024 presidential race when facing obstacles. The combination of acknowledged barriers + decision delay + subsequent media silence + lack of urgency + historical precedent makes a pre-July 1 announcement unlikely.
